reviews.review_description: As a musician, audio engineer, performer, and all around nerd, I'm blown away at all of the things that PatchWorld will let me create, from instruments that make sound based off body movements, to interactive augmented reality experiences, it's hard to decide what to do first!

Patchworld is a virtual, collaborative, immersive sandbox and playground where you can create virtual spaces, instruments, drum machines, functional props, shooting galleries, airplanes, and even roller coasters using a plug n play system of logic, function blocks, audiovisual assets, and more.

You will encounter some bugs but it’s being actively developed, devs are eager to get feedback, a massive update was just released, and it’s coming to Steam/PC, which should bring in a larger community.

Build, jam, share, and check out community creations. A great tool for coders, virtual creators, music makers, and gamers, too. An amazing educational tool that merges math and music. Where we go from here is a choice I leave to you.

Great App! Let your imagination run wild. People do talk and hang out but Im not seeing it at night as much as I used to.  Its very interesting app with Music, animation, art, puppetry, making gadgets that run and control these objects.

You start off creating a world and from there you can either start working on visuals or leave it a solid color and start building Patches. 

<=
just 2 hours in, but in love:) thx guys👍🖖

The limits are endless! This game has a strong community of music professionals and casual jammers. 

Its not just a game, it works with your Daw. Ableton link works without issue (but a little getting used to)

If you are not sure if it's possible, it is! Join the discord.
Many ready to go new virtual instruments, which makes playing music inspiring and easy for anyone, from curious to musicians

Nothing else to say, but awesome. 

Please look up Patchworld in YouTube to get a real feel for it. 
Patchworld is very unique in that you can build devices and instruments, as well as use already custom built devices to make you own music. 
I choose to make music. Here is a simple but effective way to create your own music and I am not music minded.
https://youtu.be/0XOgiPrvHss?si=2CpIekz1qJuuEkb4, or view @MickW-p3o

reviews.review_title: One app to rule them all
reviews.review_description:
Do you like playing instruments knowing or not knowing music? This is your app. 

Alive and overwhelming community! 

I'm using Patchworld connected with Touchdesigner and Ableton, and this is and endless path of posibilities.
I went in just playing on stuff but ended up creating things i never thought i had in me. This is easily the most fun and most rewarding use i have for my headset!
Can i get my money back though

This is an amazing VR experience for anyone who enjoys music. Weather you are a musician or just someone who loves music this will make you smile. It's like Jim Henson made a VR music app. 

I downloaded this after dropping a tab. The intro was a blast, the characters are all polite and encouraging. Then when I started building a world and making music, going to the different pre built worlds I was fully immersed and turned my bedroom into a soundscape of beats and loops that provided a backdrop to one of the most mentally stimulatimg trips I've ever had.
I revisited the program a few days later and had just as much fun without the acid as I did with it.

It's not simple. You need to pay attention to the tutorials and I wouldn't mind if they were a little more detailed. Maybe if you had a list of questions to select from and DJ patchee could elaborate on the subject? Obviously different folks will have different areas they need guidance on. But that's not a complaint by any means. It's a beautiful, user friendly, satisfying program. It's well worth the price and if you like music you're gonna have an amazing time in this world. Multi-player potentially could be groundbreaking. 

In short I can't praise this development team enough for making a VR experience that really embraces the experience VR provides and takes full advantage of the possibilities.

This app should draw in any musician out there… especially anyone familiar with electronic instruments. If you have an understanding of signal flow… you can do that too. Tons of prebuilt devices and the option to build your own. Everything is patchable. So if you want to hear what happens when you control or trigger sounds and fx with pretty much whatever you want. Get experimental. See if you can find out what it can’t do. 

There’s the option to import your own WAV, OGG, M4V, GLB, PNG, JPG assets to personalize things. With control over the scene’s lighting, particle effects, background, terrain, objects you take it further. Blow people’s minds by then connecting your music to the lights and the background because that’s patchable too.

But there’s also an advanced section that has things like logic components, math, converters and even rotors to make a robot or other moving parts. It’s really a safe space to tinker, tweak and build stuff. 

The community is top notch, and a very active discord community. It’s easy to get help, get questions answered. A lot of people in there really want to make music with others because it’s easy. You can easily join a live session or make your private session live and see if anyone shows up. 

It’s a bit more than just a music app. It’s got a bit of world building and metaverse aspect to it as well as some physics simulation. Just the other day I was sequencing some drum samples… one person in the room was building a robot, someone else was connect math parts to bend a light beam. 

If you want to do something more mentally and creatively engaging with your quest, check it out.

The level of effort that has gone into this app is seriously impressive and many other developers should take note. I'd been hesitating at the full price but at the sale price that's on as I write this, it's an absolute steal. Great fun for anyone but especially for the musically-inclined. Just buy it and enjoy!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We have many more great things coming!

reviews.review_description: Patchworld is hands-down one of the best things I’ve tried in VR. It’s a super fun, creative space where you can make music, visuals, and anything in between. The community is amazing, welcoming and supportive, and everyone is treated with respect whether you’re just starting out or already experienced. I 100% recommend it to anyone.

I have been trying to learn how to make sounds on daws on and off for over a decade. This gives me real hope, it is so much fun and completely absorbing. It's got me watching videos about sampling theorem before I go to sleep.

I was listening to some music since exploring patch, and I started to visualize the electronic production in my minds eye in a new way.

There was an acid techno room and it was really joyful and special to play with making the bounciesy sound I could. It's so easy to get lost in it. Really gorgeous experience. 

I started to get overwhelmed and someone very friendly explained about jolts as events and the big plugs are like signals that will keep on sending information (I am learning one little bit at a time). This is so beyond my comfort zone. But it actually makes looking at physics and data fun and flow in a way I never accessed.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing message! 🧡
We’re really happy PatchWorld gave you a fresh way to explore sound and have fun learning . There is for sure something extremely satisfying to learn about complex physics, by holding the parts in your hands... 

You will learn a lot also on Discord : https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr

Keep patching and enjoying the ride! ✨

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love making music, and this has been such a fun and social experience! If you enjoy experimenting with different instruments, layering sounds, and recording your own samples, this is a must have. On top of that, the visuals and art style are full of personality, and look great. It really adds to the overall vibe and makes the whole experience super enjoyable.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review!! We hope to see you at one of our community events! Happy Patching!!

reviews.review_description: A true sandbox of continuous development, create a world, create a machine, create music!

I've used this app for all of 30 minutes, I tried the trial. And then started experimenting and instantly purchased once my trial was over, incredibly creative and awesome concept that honestly I feel is what digital music production needs. Being huddled over a laptop feels restrictive already after just using this app for just 30 minutes. Incredible work to the developers.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! dont forget to join our community on discord to get the most out of it!

reviews.review_title: Way more than a music app
reviews.review_description: After two weeks with Patchworld I'm stumped as to how to describe it other than one of the most engaging VR experiences I've ever had. Jam with friends, design instruments, play with its full-featured visual programming  environment... all of that, but a lot more. Combine all of that with a friendly community and the feeling that the devs had as much fun putting it together as I've had playing with it, and you won't find another app with a better entertainment-to-dollar ratio.

developer_response.body: THANKS for the review!! We are equally excited to have creators like you on board !

reviews.review_description: I personally don't do much with music but it's  definitely got a lot of that stuff here.  I'm definitely here because I love to tinker, build things and experiment with things.  There's plenty of that here too.  It's  not just music and DJs.   It's still growing  and updating.   You have to check it out for yourself.

developer_response.body: You have been really doing amazing work in PatchWorld!!

reviews.review_description: Highly recommend checking out Todd's weekly workshop on Tuesday mornings (join the official patch world discord to find your timezone), always learn a lot of cool stuff there!  If you're a tinkerer or a musician there's endless possibility with this to make creative audio visual experiences to share with the world, or if you want to just be a casual observer who checks out other people's cool creations, all are welcome!

As a sound artist, after finding out Patchworld i got interested and started a research project about sound&VR. 
A LOT of possibilities (in visual also) - great, friendly community and prompt updates makes Patchworld a must for everyone interested a creative and fun way to use the VR. 
A couple of friends tested out, and now we are about to form a VR band :) Great fun online, and irl with a good PA and some room to wobble around ;)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, How is your VR band called?

reviews.review_description: patch world has not ever worked ￼￼ I have uninstalled it reinstalled it. I have uninstalled it from the headset and installed it through the app Verizon’s app. I have tried to open it up without the Wi-Fi attached I have I’ve done everything I possibly could without having any luck, please help me. I spent 30 bucks on this and I’m very excited to use it and it does not work, I’ve done everything besides factory reset my headset which I don’t want to do help help help help

developer_response.body:
Hope your issue was fixed since you left that review... We have released a few updates improving a lot of things.

reviews.review_title: Best vr music playground out there
reviews.review_description: Tons of modular synth blocks, effects, visuals, asset import (wav,glb,avi etc), all wrapped up in a fun explorable way, with one of THE best (and funniest) onboarding tutorial sets in any game

developer_response.body: Thanks for saying that! we are making a lot of improvements on the blocks too! and your feedbacks are so important to help us make the best VR creation tool out there!

reviews.review_title: The era of VJing (virtual Jockey) begins
reviews.review_description:
I come with history... Started off with the Amiga demo scene... Then VJO; underground realtime Visuals software. We now enter the era of the VJ (Virtual Jockey) and Patchwork will be the platform birthing this movement.

Can't call it an app, it's so much more. It's a platform for modular systhesis of Music and Visuals.
Well done. Bloody brilliant! Buy it.

Will adjust if I can get this working    quest 3 won't record audio to make bubbles.... but other than that games real nifty and fun to play... anybody else have this issue or do dev have a fix?

EDIT: 10 DAYS IN...no reply from devs.....very said...will give it 72 hrs before I req a refund

developer_response.body:
Hey did you managed to fix your mic issue? If not, please join our discord so we can tackle that together. 
https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr .

I don’t know why the decision was made to have them all talk like they are babies or whatever the vibe is going for but they are actually so annoying it almost makes the tutorial unplayable. I can’t be the only person who thinks this 😭

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback JankyDoodlez, we have fixed the rain maker issue a while back., and made a lot of improvement since your comments. We hope you 'll come back and enjoy the progress. have a look at the hub, now we have the c ommunity section, where you can explore, and remix what the community has been making!!. 

Come join our community on discord,  I promise you will not feel like a baby :) (https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr)  Hope to see you there!✌🏽
(for the story, the characters are indeed GAD 's baby voices )

reviews.review_description: This game really allows you to see the utility of the metaverse. It is wild to me how these instruments exist in this overlying world, and you can even connect it to Ableton. The team updates the game and has an active discord community. Check it out! Totally worth the buy!

I love going on musical sessionn in with this app and really nice tracks come out of it.
There is a learning curve, but it's not so hard once you do the tutorials in the app.
Thank you for this :)

reviews.review_title: Seems really powerful, a blast as well
reviews.review_description: Its really fun bringing lots of different pieces together to make music. There's lots to learn, but the quirky help character is kinda charming. It gets you up to speed pretty quick, but there's lots to explore once you get grounded. I'm looking forward to actually making some things now, if only I can stop myself from just playing for fun

I love it wow endless possibilities with music. I come back after a month gone and Everything Thing I could wish for is here. The new Recording Box, the new premade world is amazing. I can’t believe this, I wasted 119.00 on a Xbox game because I can’t take the quest off now. This patchworld done it for me. I would by the quest just for this.

developer_response.body: Cool, Glad you appreciate our latest updates... More to come

reviews.review_title: About how much I love this game.
reviews.review_description: I’ve been playing this for so long. It’s been so much fun and it’s really fun I just can’t stop I can’t stop playing it is just really want it so bad I really want to make a world but I don’t know how so yeah.

developer_response.body:
hey Crank5759, come to our discord if you want some help to create your own world!!
https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr

reviews.review_description:
This “game” is the closest thing to a VR digital audio workstation I’ve found on the quest and I can see it becoming something special. 
I like how the environment becomes a musical visualiser and the characters that guide you are actually funny. Very creative concepts; it’s definitely worth checking out!

developer_response.body: Thx @70him, patworld is only at the beginning of its journey, a lot is cooking, and will be released this year. Looking forward to see what you create!

reviews.review_description: This blew my mind right from the get go.  It's the most immersive tutorial I've ever seen in any medium.  I really felt like I was there hanging out with these weird alien dudes.  So much personality!  Anyway, it's not really a game, more like a engine for creating musical and visual experiences.  There is nothing else like it and I can't wait to spend more time in it and make my own worlds.  To the devs, you guys are awesome!  I hope to see more of my new "friends" in the future!

developer_response.body: :) and we hope to see what you will be creating!!! By the way if you are not already, you should get into our discord, we are gonna give a workshop in the next days about how to record your own alien dudes to populate the app to more fun tutorial!!  dont miss it!

reviews.review_title: Love this ! The new ableton integration
reviews.review_description:
The new ableton max4live
Device is a game
Changer . I can’t believe I can send and receive commands from any OSC supporting platform
One day 

Super fun worlds to explore and it’s possible to patch your own . Can’t wait to see where this goes

developer_response.body: MORE integration coming up in the next few day, stay tune!!!

reviews.review_description: As a computational mathematician of a certain age, I remember searching through long lines of code for an error 🤬. So I really appreciate the ease at which these models can be constructed.

developer_response.body:
Music is math without counting :)

Thanks for the note. We are really glad to read you here and looking forward to hearing you in PatchWorld!

reviews.review_title: Perfect for educators in VR + STEM
reviews.review_description:
This app is one of the best examples in VR of creative collaboration through the lens of music. Empathetic characters show you how to play the instruments and adapt to you if you are having trouble with a concept ( I was a little confused at first by how to play some of the instruments but the interactive characters actually guide you step by step ). 

At first it might seem like fun and games - but as you progress it becomes clear that there is a whole deeper world under the surface that can build real world skills + critical thinking + design and creativity, all through visual programming blocks and wiring together "Patch" cables. Engaging students is really one of the biggest challenges in today's STEM/STEAM classrooms and what I love about the theme of music + creativity is that it is something every student can relate to.  Creating and sharing interactive musical worlds with each other in VR is also a perfect way to a creative design class remotely since students can log into their headsets from home/lab/school and can send virtual worlds and recordings to each other asynchronously almost like google docs - but in 3D and way more engaging.

Will be looking forward to exploring how the mixed-reality (MR) side works as well - especially since this mode blends more of the real world in with the virtual.

Highly recommend to just give it a try and see for yourself - and perhaps get a glimpse at the future of learning and education in VR !

reviews.review_title: One of the best games on Quest
reviews.review_description: I was looking for a new way to experience music in vr, and this I better than every other music game on the platform.  The intuitive tools make an easy to learn yet also proffessional way of creating sounds.  And the tutorial system is one of the smartest ideas I've ever seen, it's like there's a person in the world with you, guiding you through it.  The in game people are the same as you, holding controllers, moving the same way you do, its incredible.  I haven't played a vr game in a while that made me giddy with excitement, but I find a huge smile across my face as I bop and dance to an new beat I made.  Best game on Quest!!!

This music creation tool is so much more than you’d expect. 

It’s an extremely versatile musical instrument, in fact, multiple instruments. The tutorial is extremely entertaining and brilliant. Not just for making music, it’s clearly designed for performing and taking part in music and jamming with others.

It still has some way to go to add polish to the user experience. The first 30 mins were great and I was jamming and playing and creating and felt brilliant. But then I was overwhelmed when I accidentally opened a menu and there was just too much stuff I didn’t understand and it broke the smoothness of the onboarding experience I had started with. It would be good to be introduced to all those options later maybe. 

From the options screen you could seemingly spawn almost anything. This means you could create loads of stuff that made the app lag.

But this is clearly in development and I’m excited to see how it develops. It has the potential to become a serious creative social musical metaverse all on its own - Meta take note! You should support this one with all you got.
Thanks for bringing new tools to expand possibilities for networking in music performance! All the best and see you in the VR jam ! :)

and you might want this

https://www.oculus.com/appreferrals/loseWeight/3715150718552632/?utm_source=2

reviews.review_title: This is a revolution! I love it.
reviews.review_description:
This app will totally blow your mind. It redefines relationship with space and music in VR.
It’s the whole package, creative and artistic, interactive and social, collaborative and evolutive.
The creators are setting new standards and locomotion is super smart.
Congratulations and thank you to team.
Let’s start patching.

developer_response.body:
Yeeee baby!!! You can see the vision, SOO happy to read this, cant wait to see what you patch! Join our Discord and drop me a line, can feature some of your work if you send us some stuff, more info patchxr.com

You rock, thanks for the love <3
Gad - product manager at PatchXR

reviews.review_description: As someone who's played with synths, drum machines, MPC pads and tons more, Patch is super intuitive and tons of fun. It has a light-hearted level of humour right in the beginning but quickly scales up to an incredibly powerful and immersive creative audio tool. Your imagination can run wild and make any sounds you can dream of. I definitely think this is one of the best games out there to stretch the creative muscles 💪

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the smile you gave us while reading your comment. 

We cant wait to hear what you make. Will strongly recommend the check next week's update. There is a big chance you will like it.

reviews.review_title: There is nothing like that out there!
reviews.review_description: From the bottom of my hearth, patchworld is the best music software out there. I'm not just taking about vr or oculus, but in general. I've spent many years playing performative and modular music, generative programmed randomic sounds, and before that, I've never met an app capable of melt in a so intuitive way the performatic approach with a solid engine to create any kind of instrument and music system. The graphic is smooth, the movement system is perfect, the passtrough option is ready for future xr development. Great app for learning modular synthesis. Finally we can use a professional tool also in quest, and not just basic "music games" like before. I'm very happy to support the team, they are very kind and open to suggestion and criticism. Thank you so much!

developer_response.body: Thx a lot Alessio for your positive comments. We are doing our best to keep-up to your expectations!!

reviews.review_title: The sound is lagging after 5 minutes off
reviews.review_description: How do I get a refund... Selling ur bs game at this cost is crazy

developer_response.body: Hi Beau_Lieu, thx for reporting that you are having a problem with our app, we have not had that complain before so I think it can be fixed easily, but we need more information to help you resolve this issue. We have a support channel on discord, please join so we can see what's going on. And as far as refund, easy, In the Purchase History menu, you will see a list of the most recent software you  purchased. Tap the purchase to be refunded, and hit Request Refund.

reviews.review_description: I downloaded the trial version of the game because it sounded interesting and I was not disappointed. I thought it was just another music creation tool, but when I tried it I was surprised at how unique it is. While yes it is a music creation tool, it is probably the best one out there. I loved how much charm and character it has and how unique each of the tutorial characters are, they all have their own unique personality and style of music. The game gives you sound clips you can use to change what an instrument sounds like but it also lets you record your own (which is really cool). I am not the biggest music fan and know almost nothing about how to play instrument, but I could still manage to make songs that sound decent. I have tried and I think it is nearly impossible to make something that sounds bad (even just hitting random notes). I loved the techno music world because it was a lot of fun to mess with the beat makers. I give this game a 10/10 and think everyone should try it.

This is the most entertaining/comprehensive music making app I've played! It will have you lolling at the crazy funny characters. Who all sound like someone you would meet in a chill out room. Then there's the crazy but really useful and inspiring music making tools. 
The bouncing beat world will mate your feel like you've just stepped into a90s dreamscape flyer. The moment the free demo ended I immediately purchased the game? £15 what a bargain.

I've only scraped the serface of this app but it has so much potential.

I'll do an update when I've spent more time on it.

for the past few days the only vr game i've been playing is patchworld. i went through each tutorial at least twice and loved it. i only had to watch 1 tutorial and I was already making my own patches. I messed around with just the Marble Rain pack for hours, it reminds me a lot of Animusic's Pipe Dream. Also I found out you can attach an object to your hands or head and I turned myself into a Goron from Majora's Mask. This game is worth every penny, please buy it, you'll love it.

developer_response.body: Hey WillisCraft101, thx for the feedback, where can we see your Mask?? please share some selfies in the creator showcase channel on discord!!!

reviews.review_description: People who made this piece of art are absolutely genius. One thing I could wish is more aps from them...

developer_response.body: HAHA, thx, well PatchWorld still keeps us very busy... We are releasing update regularly and we have still a lot we want to achieve with this one... But we made one beautiful experience called the jellyfish that you should be able to find on Applab :)

reviews.review_title: A new kind of creative tool
reviews.review_description:
Patchworld is, essentially, the VR equivalent to node based audio(&visual) softwares like Max 8, Puredata, Supercollider etc. That, in itself, makes it quite an incredible achievement already. For those unfamiliar with what "node based" or "patching" means : it means you get little "blocks" that can do simple functions (musical and visual functions like oscillators, filters, speakers, multipliers, 2D sprites, 3D meshes, movements, and the list goes on there's A LOT in there) and get to plug them to each other in all the ways you can think of to create whatever you can imagine. For some, there might be a learning curve, where others (eurorack enthusiast, coders, Max/Puredata folks etc.) will just feel right at home. Either way learning is rewarding, and VR helps a lot to make sense with the blocks.

But to me what can't be understated is just how incredible it is to sit there in your "patch" and watch it evolve as you work, physically, around you. Building an instrument and seeing it take shape, being able to reorganize the way sound is spatialized in real time with no limits to the amount of speakers you can set up, effectively creating a simulation for a massive installation. Being used to patching in traditional "2D environnements" there's just no equivalent that I can think of.

The other exciting aspect is the evolution of the software towards a complete audiovisual tool where you can easily integrate audio and visuals to create synergy using your audio data to drive visuals. With a limited set of functions, so much is already possible, and with more on the way, it's quickly becoming a sort of barebone creative interface to build little game-like experiences, constrained to simple features, but it's one of those cases where making it simple just opens up creativity by removing a lot of things to worry about.

All in all, like any creative tool it requires patience and learning, but it makes a terrific job at being accessible, rewarding and incredibly open ended.

developer_response.body:
Hi LLK! Thank you so much, you are the cream of the crop when it comes to patching skills, PatchWorld is all based on amazing creators like yourself. We can't wait to bring your latest works to the rest of the world!

See you on Discord!
Gad

reviews.review_description:
An amazing app to experiment creatively with sound and vision. 

The tutorials are engaging and facilitate a seamless transition into PatchWorld. I have used Apple GarageBand 2D virtual instruments but with PatchWorld instruments, I feel I am in the music, inspiring many other enjoyable ways of playing.By offering import options for custom visuals and audio, the scope and flexibility to build instruments and worlds is endless. 

I just hope when the PC version releases it is cross-buy so I can harness the power of my PC.

developer_response.body:
Hi Paulinvr7, so good to read such a comment! WHat you describe is exactly what we are are going for, making music creation more fun and playful, and at the same time expended by limitless possibilities with world building..  
We would love to see and hear what you are making with PatchWorld. Please join our discord server_ https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr 
We can also chat about our PC beta version ! :) :) :)

reviews.review_description: The sound lags big time, and the sound is distorted/crunchy. I had high hopes for this game

developer_response.body: Hey Cannamink, Sorry to hear you are having some lagging issues, are you using bluetooth headphones or connection? Please join our our discord server so we can give you support (https://discord.gg/gwMVhKWxbr) We should be able to resolve this

reviews.review_title: Bought as soon as trial ended!
reviews.review_description: I am in love with this app. I bought it immediately after completing the demo and haven't stopped playing it since. It's so relaxing and beautiful. The pre-made environments are fantastic but I also love the ability to create my own customized worlds to play music in. I can't wait to see where the developers take this app because it's already stunning! I've never been so quick to purchase an app after completing a demo as I was for this one.

developer_response.body: So great to hear your having a great time with the app, join us on discord!!

reviews.review_description: Can.'t wait for the release of multiplayer. Saw some footage and it looks so fun

reviews.review_title: worth it worth it worth it
reviews.review_description: only regret I have is not discovering it sooner.

reviews.review_title: This is not a game!!!! This is 10/10!!!
reviews.review_description:
Upfront disclaimer: Do not get this App if your looking for a occasional pick it up shootem zombie game or an arcade bouncing experience, sorry this isn’t that. Nor is this a ducking and diving, trying to match the beat of someone else’s musical track rhythm game. 
It’s much, much more deeper then that… 

Let me repeat, THIS IS NOT GAME. 

That being said, This may be the greatest app I have ever seen, Exploring physics, sight, and sound. This app challenges you to think outside of your norm, to go into the weird, to disconnect from what we call reality and dive feet first into the unknown. Don’t believe me? Just try it for 10 minutes…. I promise that you will see a glimpse into the future…
Good journey patcher, hope to see you in your world

developer_response.body: Great comment G10TT1!! You are totally right, Although PatchWorld is very playful at start, it is really a powerful creative tool for playing with sound and visual in unique ways! Thx for bringing that up! Have fun patching !!!

reviews.review_description:
So many things that you can do, it’s just a matter of how creative can you get! I loved the underwater world + the desert one, it’s really entertaining for non-musicians, but the real potential here seems to be the possibility of actually building your own worlds, like I did back in the day with fortnite. I joined the early community on discord, and it looks like this is just the beginning! Totally worth it!

Can't wait for next releases!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! You are right its gonna be a crazy ride! See you on Discord!!! XD

reviews.review_title: This is the future of music!
reviews.review_description: This is the future of music! Musical worlds you can inhabit, interact with and become a part of. A completely psychedelic, immersive experience. I love it!

developer_response.body: Cheers :)

reviews.review_description: IT s SO cool you can make your own music you cam make your own Sounds create things clone yourself you can do everything in this game But i wish that was more fluid instrument ESP
If this gets expanded on the way the devs promise, this is going to be huge.

developer_response.body:
Thank you soooo much for the review <3
Come to Discord to get in touch with us to show your work, and get latest updates, tips & tricks:
